bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, and the opponent’s opening offer gives me the minimum value on every issue. I am targeting maximum utility with a strong opening counteroffer, while staying within the valid domain.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), so there is little need to concede much yet. The opponent has moved slightly toward my preferences on issueA, but their offers are still very low utility for me, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid around 0.968 because it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has shown almost no meaningful concession, even reverting to their initial low-utility offer. I will concede only on my lowest-weight issue, issueD, while holding firm on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.06, and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me with only minor movement on issueA before reverting. Since issueD has the lowest weight for me, I will continue making small concessions there while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A, B, and C.

I targeted a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low for me, alternating between utility about 0.20 and 0.26 with little real concession. I am continuing to concede only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low for me, only fluctuating between all valueF and a small concession on issueA. Since issueD has the lowest weight for me, I am conceding there first while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A, B, and C. The opponent has not shown meaningful sustained concession yet, so there is no reason to make large concessions now.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.89 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly between all-F and issueA=valueE with the rest at valueF, so their offers remain very low utility to me and show limited concession. I will continue conceding first on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.856 because it is early in the negotiation, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and show only minimal movement on issueA, so I am continuing gradual concessions on my lower-weight issues rather than weakening issueA or issueB. Since my previous bid conceded issueD fully and issueC slightly, I now concede issueC one more step while holding my most important issues firm.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.824 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me, alternating between all valueF and only a small concession on issueA. Since they have not shown meaningful concession, I will continue a gradual concession path while protecting my highest-weight issues, especially issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.79, since it is early at t=0.18 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me with only slight movement on issueA. Their behavior suggests they are mostly holding firm, so I will continue conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while protecting issueA and issueB.

I targeted a still-strong utility bid around 0.76, preserving my highest-weight issues A and B while conceding fully on the lower-weight issues C and D. Time is only at 0.20, so there is no need to make major concessions on the important issues yet, especially since the opponent’s offers remain very low for me and show little real movement. There are no opponent messages to factor in.

At t=0.22 I am still targeting a relatively strong utility, but I need to show movement because the opponent has remained very firm around valueE/valueF offers that are low for me. I have already conceded the lowest-weight issues C and D substantially, so the next concession is a small step on issueB while holding issueA, my most important issue, at valueA. The opponent’s offers suggest they prefer lower values, but they have not made meaningful concessions beyond occasionally moving issueA to valueE.

At t=0.24 I am still targeting a relatively high utility around 0.70, since there is ample time left and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me, only alternating between all valueF and a small improvement on issueA. To avoid simply repeating my last bid and to test whether movement on issueA is more valuable to them, I concede on issueA while restoring issueB, which is still important to my utility.

I am targeting a utility around 0.66: still well above reservation and appropriate for t=0.26, while making a further concession from my prior bids. The opponent’s valid offers remain very low for me and show little real concession beyond sometimes moving issueA to valueE, so I will keep my most important issueA at valueA and concede further on issueB after already conceding issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.65, which is a modest concession from my prior offers while still preserving value on the most important issues. At t=0.28 there is no need to collapse toward the opponent’s very low offers yet, especially since their bids have mostly stayed at valueF with only occasional movement on issueA to valueE. Since issueC and issueD are lower-weight for me and the opponent keeps asking for valueF there, I concede those while making smaller concessions on issueA and issueB.

At t=0.30 I am still targeting a fairly strong utility around 0.69 rather than rushing to the opponent’s very low offers, since their bids remain mostly fixed at valueF with only occasional movement on issueA. I am conceding mainly on lower/medium-weight issues while holding my highest-weight issueA at valueA, and I avoid repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.65, conceding further from my last offer while still protecting the highest-weight issueA. The opponent has barely conceded, repeatedly offering valueF on most issues and only sometimes valueE on issueA, so I am not treating their bids as strong evidence of flexibility; at t=0.32 there is still time, but some concession is appropriate.

I am targeting a utility around 0.63, which is a moderate concession from my earlier high-utility bids but still protects my most important issues at t=0.34. The opponent has mostly held firm at very low-utility offers for me, occasionally moving only issueA to valueE, so I should not over-concede yet; I am varying the tradeoff by conceding some on issueA while keeping issueB/issueC from falling too far.

I am targeting a utility a little above 0.60, making a modest concession from my previous bid while preserving issueA because it has the highest weight for me. At t=0.36 there is still time, and the opponent’s offers have mostly stayed extremely low for me, with only occasional movement on issueA, so I should not collapse toward their preferred all-F pattern yet.

I targeted a utility around 0.56, which is a meaningful concession from my prior offer while still well above the opponent’s very low offers to me. At t=0.38 there is time pressure to start moving, but not enough to collapse to their repeated valueE/valueF-heavy bids; their history shows minimal concession and no messages to justify trusting any stronger claim.

I targeted a utility a little above 0.55, continuing gradual concessions without dropping close to the opponent's very low offers. At t=0.40 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their near-reservation bids; their offers remain almost fixed at valueF on all issues, with only occasional movement to valueE on issueA, so I concede further while protecting overall utility.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.56, continuing to concede mainly on lower-weight issues while protecting issueA, my highest-weight issue. The opponent has held nearly firm at very low-utility bids for me, only occasionally moving issueA from valueF to valueE, so I should not make a large late-style concession yet at t=0.42. This bid avoids repeating my exact previous offer while signaling additional flexibility on issueB.

At t=0.44 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.50 rather than dropping to the opponent’s very low offers, since there is still time before the deadline. The opponent has mostly held firm at issueB/issueC/issueD = valueF and only occasionally conceded issueA to valueE, so I am keeping some value on high-weight issueA while conceding the other issues they consistently demand.

I am targeting a utility around 0.46, making a real concession from my recent offers while still protecting issueA, which is my highest-weight issue. At t=0.46 we are in the mid-game, and the opponent has mostly held firm at very low-utility offers for me, so I will concede on lower/mid-weight issues first while testing whether issueA=valueB is acceptable.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.39 by conceding further on the contested high-weight issueA while keeping the offer better than the opponent’s repeated low-utility bids. At t=0.48 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their E/F/F/F pattern; their offers show they strongly prefer late values and have only minimally conceded on issueA, with no messages adding credible information.

At t=0.50, I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.328, conceding further from my prior offer while still preserving value above the opponent’s recurring offers to me. The opponent has been very firm, mostly offering all valueF or issueA:valueE with the rest valueF, so I am moving issueA one step closer to their apparent preferred region while keeping the rest already fully conceded.

I targeted a modest positive utility above the opponent’s repeated offer, while continuing to concede mainly on issueA because the opponent consistently signals wanting low values across all issues and has only moved between valueE/valueF on issueA. At t=0.52 there is meaningful time pressure but not yet a final deadline, so I avoid immediately dropping to their exact valueE/valueF demand and instead test the smallest remaining gap on the most salient issue.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.26 by matching the opponent’s repeated valid offer, which is still above reservation value. At t=0.54 the negotiation is past the midpoint, and the opponent has remained very firm around issueA=valueE with issueB/C/D=valueF, so conceding to that signaled position is the best chance to secure agreement. There are no natural-language messages to weigh, so I rely only on the offer pattern.
